name: Duke Alvarez
alt: Duke Alvarez in a sim racing workshop with wheel rims and force-feedback hardware
voice: Blunt, useful, allergic to wobbly pedal decks.
beat: Duke translates torque, pedals, cockpits, and ecosystem lock-in into buying orders that do not eat your wallet twice.

name: Val Chen
alt: Val Chen in a flight and space sim controls workshop
voice: Precise, calm, quietly devastating about bad cable routing.
beat: Val owns yokes, sticks, HOSAS layouts, head tracking, USB topology, and the difference between immersion and expensive desk clutter.

name: Nina Brooks
alt: Nina Brooks in a golf simulator room with measuring tools and launch-monitor alignment gear
voice: Warm, direct, will absolutely ask your ceiling height first.
beat: Nina turns launch monitors, mats, screens, projectors, and room dimensions into build sheets that survive real walls.

name: Mac Donovan
alt: Mac Donovan at a DIY motion and tactile feedback electronics bench
voice: Sardonic maker energy, useful wiring paranoia.
beat: Mac covers tactile feedback, motion rigs, USB hubs, power, EMI, and the small mistakes that make a cockpit act haunted.

name: Gus Calder
alt: Gus Calder in a compact ship bridge simulator workspace
voice: Dry, patient, deeply amused by repurposed racing wheels.
beat: Gus handles marine sims, oddball controllers, panels, helms, throttles, and every build that starts with 'hear me out.'
